tasks.title: Create API endpoint for sensor readings

conflicts.title: Battery-powered device using HTTP
conflicts.reason: The device uses a battery but sends data using HTTP, which may increase power consumption.
conflicts.suggestion: Use MQTT or reduce the number of requests.

conflicts.title: Low latency requirement with HTTP
conflicts.reason: The project needs fast updates, but HTTP may not be the best choice for frequent IoT messages.

conflicts.title: Sensor data without validation
conflicts.reason: Sensor values may be wrong or missing if the system does not validate the data.
conflicts.suggestion: Add validation before saving data to the database.

services.name: Backend API
services.description: Receives sensor readings from the IoT device.

services.name: Database
services.description: Stores sensor history and project data.

alerts.title: Wi-Fi signal is unstable
alerts.description: The ESP32 may disconnect if the signal remains weak.

alerts.title: Light value is higher than expected
alerts.description: Check if the light sensor is exposed to direct light.

logs.title: Temperature reading received
logs.message: ESP32 sent a new temperature value to the backend.

logs.title: Wi-Fi warning detected
logs.message: The system detected unstable communication with the gateway.

questions: Is the device connected to power and turned on?
Can you ping the device from your computer?
Does the serial monitor show any error messages?
Is the server address configured correctly in the code?
path: Check API endpoints on the server
